name: Police Car Chase - Car Racing Simulator | Car Driving

reviews.review_description: Police Chase VR is pretty fun! The chases are exciting, and upgrading cars keeps it interesting. It's not super realistic, but it's still a good time if you just want fast action and chaos. Would be awesome if they add a multiplayer mode so we can chase with friends. Overall, a fun game to jump into for some quick thrill rides!

It’s genuinely unplayable with how the grips are currently. If your right hand is holding on to the wheel and you hit brake with your left hand the your right hand will no longer be on the steering wheel anymore and you’ll never notice it. Why is the accelerate button the grip button? You’re forced to drive with your right hand because the right hand grips the wheel and it’s dominant in control. Oh yeah, I didn’t mention how there’s no difference between whether or not both your hands are on the wheel? Whatever trigger you’re currently pressing it the one that’s controlling the wheel. Why is it so that if I hit the trigger to grip the steering wheel and then hit the grip button, my hand is no longer on the wheel. Why is it so that if I grip the wheel then accelerate my hand is no longer holding the wheel? Why is it that in order to accelerate your RIGHT HAND SPECIFICALLY needs to be holding the wheel. Your game makes it impossible to even hold on to the wheel at all. If you’re holding on to the steering wheel with your right hand which you use to accelerate then hit trigger on your left hand you’re suddenly not holding the wheel on your right hand anymore. Why do either of the triggers even affect the grip at all? They should literally just move your car forwards and backwards. If you think I’m overreacting buy this garbage for yourself. I will keep this game in my collection because it was $5 when I bought and so that I can show my friends what a truly horrible driving game feels like.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your incredibly detailed feedback—we sincerely apologize for the frustrating experience you described. You’re absolutely right: the grip and trigger mechanics were un-intuitive and poorly designed in earlier versions, especially the conflict between accelerating/braking and maintaining control of the steering wheel.

We’re happy to share that we’ve completely reworked this system in our latest update:

Triggers now ONLY control acceleration/braking and no longer interfere with grip.

Grip buttons are separate from driving inputs, so your hands stay firmly on the wheel unless you intentionally release them.

Both hands now function independently, allowing true two-handed steering without unexpected disconnects.

This overhaul addresses the issues you raised (e.g., hands detaching unintentionally, right-hand dominance conflicts, and confusing trigger/grip overlap). We’d love for you to try the updated version and see if it resolves your concerns. Your specific insights were invaluable in guiding these fixes!

As an early access title, we’re committed to refining the game daily. If the changes improve your experience, we’d be grateful if you could consider revising your review to reflect the updates. Either way, please share your thoughts with us at rebelsgamestudio@gmail.com—we’re eager to hear your verdict.

Thank you for giving us a chance to make things right. We’re determined to turn this into a game you’ll want to showcase for all the right reasons.

reviews.review_description: it felt just absolutely unplayable and just what I had in mind of that game was just the complete opposite. Would not recommend wasting your money on this game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your honest feedback, and we’re deeply sorry the game didn’t meet your expectations. We completely understand your frustration, and we want you to know that we’ve just released an updated version addressing critical issues like stability, controls, and gameplay mechanics. Many of the problems you described have been resolved, and we’ve added improvements based on player feedback.

As an early access title, we’re actively refining the game daily. If you’re open to it, we’d truly appreciate you giving the latest version a try and sharing your thoughts with us at rebelsgamestudio@gmail.com. Your feedback is invaluable, and we’d love the chance to turn your experience around.

If the updates improve your experience, we’d be grateful if you could consider revising your review to reflect the changes. We’re committed to earning back your trust and delivering the game you deserve.

Thank you for your patience and support as we work to make this right.

Thank you for your feedback! We sincerely apologize for the issues you encountered. Our team has rolled out updates addressing critical bugs and improving stability to resolve the "unplayable" experience. We’ve also added new cars and features to enhance gameplay.

As an early access title, we’re constantly refining the game based on player input. Could you kindly check the latest version and share your thoughts with us at rebelsgamestudio@gmail.com? Your feedback is invaluable in shaping the future of the game.

We appreciate your patience and support as we work to deliver the best possible experience!

reviews.review_description: Fantastic environment, realistic hit effects, cool enemy cars, and controls are realistic. Action keeps you on the edge. Suggestions: Add more stunts modes. Add multiplayer mode for competitive racing & chasing with friends.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your kind words and thoughtful suggestions! We’re thrilled you’re enjoying the environment, hit effects, enemy cars, and realistic controls—it means a lot to our team.

Your ideas are awesome!

Stunt Modes: We’re already brainstorming creative stunt challenges to amp up the adrenaline.

Multiplayer Mode: Competitive racing/chasing with friends is high on our roadmap! While it’ll take time to perfect, we agree it’s a game-changer.

As an early access title, feedback like yours directly shapes our updates. If you have specific stunt ideas or multiplayer features you’d love to see, shoot us an email at rebelsgamestudio@gmail.com—we’d love to collaborate!

Thanks again for your support and for being part of this journey. Stay tuned for more thrills!
